repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: finite joint-completion LP sharpness for ADE under one shared treatment-exposure completion

published_prior_art.cite: hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference: network IV with instrumental exposure mappings; schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid: sharp misspecified treatment-exposure bounds without IV compliance
nibbering_oosterveen_2025_iv_interference_policy: policy-relevant IV direct/spillover bounds under interference, but no quotient-preserving misspecified-exposure transport frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: shared-completion ADE nonsharpness witness, but not IV-policy nontransport with compliance strata

published_prior_art.cite: auerbach_auerbach_tabord_meehan_2024_exposure_policy_relevance: policy-relevance warning, but no four-unit IV nontransport witness

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The Vazquez-Bare entry appears to cite the JASA paper actually titled 'Causal Spillover Effects Using Instrumental Variables' at 118(543):1911-1922, not the listed title/pages.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The constructive object K_g(P) is advertised as computable from observables, but Exhibit 9.1 computes it by assuming target compliers and hidden-state loads rather than deriving it from the observable law and stated primitives.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The open-class four-unit witness is not concretely exhibited: Exhibit 9.2 gives no explicit observable moment table, potential outcomes, or completion enumeration showing the same P and theta with different tau_g.

novelty_flags.label: upgrade
novelty_flags.one_line: PN2 generalization is only partially delivered: the routine LP widens the parent setting, but the flagship policy-transport delta still depends on uncomputed K_g(P) and an underspecified witness; delta_summary overstates the strict upgrade.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The target normalization is ambiguous: kappa(s,t;G_Z,D) does not condition on or weight the S=s cell although theta_CD and theta_CS use omega conditioned on S=s,T=t.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch calls the endpoint problem an LP while also optimizing over binary principal-stratum/take-up variables; it must either enumerate strata/completions or spell out a valid linearization.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: tau_g(P) is never defined, and a two-coordinate K_g(P) cannot represent a generic deterministic policy unless the policy is restricted to one exposure cell or the vector is expanded over all cells.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The parent corner and new IV/exposure layer are present, but the flagship generalization is not yet delivered because the transport object and nontransport witness are still underspecified.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define tau_g explicitly and replace K_g(P) in R^2 with either a cell-indexed coefficient array or a stated single-cell policy restriction.
Add a full four-unit table for Exhibit 9.2: pi, G_Y/G_Z candidates, D_i(z,t,r), Y_i(d,s,r), m(P), delta(P), theta, and both tau_g values.
Repair the LP statement by enumerating principal strata before the LP, or by introducing auxiliary variables and constraints that make the claimed linear program exact.
Correct the Vazquez-Bare bibliography metadata and keep the existing claim, which is substantively accurate for the verified JASA IV-spillover paper.

recommended_next_step: Revise the mathematical object definitions and worked witness before another novelty pass; the kernel is not dead, but it is below the flagship floor as written.
